verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but extramitochondrial localization including plasma membrane display is documented, with levels increasing during inflammation and tumorigenesis. Immunogold EM on tumor cells shows HSP60 at the plasma-cell membrane, and surface-proteome profiling of cancer cells identifies it among surface chaperones. The cancer-cell ecto-pool reaches the outer leaflet under a non-baseline cell state, qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility rather than no.
